expectations.text: Includes register command with correct username 'testuser' and pin '5678'
expectations.evidence: Contains 'go run . register testuser 5678'

expectations.text: Includes send command targeting alice with correct message text
expectations.evidence: Contains 'go run . send alice "hello from testuser"'

expectations.text: Commands use 'go run .' invocation (or built binary) from cli/ directory
expectations.evidence: Uses 'go run .' throughout; mentions running from cli/ directory

expectations.text: Register command comes before send command
expectations.evidence: Step 2 is register, Step 3 is send — correct ordering

expectations.text: Includes edit command with index 3
expectations.evidence: Contains 'go run . edit 3 "your corrected text here"'

expectations.text: Includes react command with index 1 and thumbs-up emoji
expectations.evidence: Contains 'go run . react 1 👍'

expectations.text: Correctly explains that read was already done (does not require re-running read)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: 'Since you already ran read alice, the index from that session is still active — you can use it directly for both operations.'

expectations.text: Provides correct go run . invocation style
expectations.evidence: Uses 'go run .' for all commands

expectations.text: Includes 'config set server_url' with the correct URL
expectations.evidence: Contains 'go run . config set server_url https://messaging.example.com'

expectations.text: Includes a verification command (config get or config get server_url)
expectations.evidence: Shows both 'go run . config get server_url' and 'go run . config get'

expectations.text: Uses correct key name 'server_url' (not 'server-url' or 'serverUrl')
expectations.evidence: Uses 'server_url' consistently

expectations.text: Explains that the setting is persistent (saved to config file)
expectations.evidence: States: 'This writes the URL to the config file at ~/.agent-messenger/config and all subsequent commands will use it automatically'
